title: phaseolus sublobatus var. setulosus
intro: Phaseolus sublobatus var. setulosus, commonly known as the hairy bean or setulose kidney bean, is a variety of legume that belongs to the genus Phaseolus and is native to tropical Asia. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its various health benefits, such as reducing blood sugar levels, lowering cholesterol, and aiding in digestion. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The plant is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to promote overall health and well-being, and its leaves, seeds, and roots are consumed separately for their nutritional and medicinal value.
